name: chromem-go
tagline: Embeddable in-memory vector database for Go with a Chroma-like interface
seoTitle: chromem-go: Embeddable Vector DB for Go
metaDescription: chromem-go is an embeddable, in-memory vector database for Go with a Chroma-like API and zero third-party dependencies. Add RAG to your app without a separate service.
overview: chromem-go is a vector database written in Go that you embed directly inside your application, the same way you would use SQLite instead of running PostgreSQL or MySQL. It stores documents and their embeddings in memory, with optional persistence, and has zero third-party dependencies.

It is aimed at Go developers who want to add retrieval augmented generation (RAG), text and code search, recommendations, or classification to an app without standing up and maintaining a separate database service. Its API mirrors Chroma's core interface, so the create-collection, add, and query flow is familiar, while also offering more Go-idiomatic methods.

Within the embedded / in-process vector database category, chromem-go trades very large scale and a wide feature set for simplicity and good performance on common workloads. The README reports querying 1,000 documents in about 0.3 ms and 100,000 documents in about 40 ms on a mid-range 2020 laptop CPU. Note that it is in beta and may introduce breaking changes before v1.0.0.
features: Embeddable like SQLite: no client-server model and no separate database to run or maintain
Chroma-like interface with CreateCollection, GetOrCreateCollection, Add, and Query methods
Built-in embedding support for multiple providers and models, so you can pass plain text and let it create embeddings (or supply your own)
Metadata and document-content filters on queries, plus concurrent adds via AddConcurrently

gettingStarted.intro: Install the module, then create a database, add documents to a collection, and run a nearest-neighbor query.

steps.heading: Install
steps.body: Add chromem-go to your Go module.

steps.heading: Create a DB and collection
steps.body: Set up an in-memory database and a collection to hold your documents.

steps.heading: Add documents and query
steps.body: Add documents (embeddings are created for you when you pass nil), then query for the most similar results. Optional metadata and content filters are supported.
steps.code:
_ = collection.Add(ctx,
    []string{"doc1", "doc2"}, // unique ID for each doc
    nil,                       // embeddings handled automatically, or supply your own
    []map[string]string{{"source": "notion"}, {"source": "google-docs"}},
    []string{"This is document1", "This is document2"},
)

results, _ := collection.Query(ctx,
    "This is a query document",
    2,
    map[string]string{"metadata_field": "is_equal_to_this"}, // optional filter
    map[string]string{"$contains": "search_string"},          // optional filter
)

useCases: Add retrieval augmented generation (RAG) or question answering to a Go app without running a separate vector database service
Build text or code search over a local document set inside a single binary
Power recommendation features from embeddings stored in-process
Run classification or clustering experiments where embedding millions of documents is not required
